1118→    @Suppress("deprecation") // onActivityResult
1119→    @Deprecated("Use Activity Result API")
1120→    override fun onActivityResult(requestCode: Int, resultCode: Int, data: Intent?) {
1121→        Timber.d("onActivityResult() with request/result: %s/%s", requestCode, resultCode)
1122→        super.onActivityResult(requestCode, resultCode, data)
1123→        if (resultCode == DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR) {
1124→            closeNoteEditor(DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR, null)
1125→        }
1126→        when (requestCode) {
1127→            REQUEST_ADD -> {
1128→                if (resultCode != RESULT_CANCELED) {
1129→                    changed = true
1130→                }
1131→            }
1132→            @NeedsTest("test to guard against changes in the REQUEST_MULTIMEDIA_EDIT clause preventing text fields to be updated")
1133→            REQUEST_MULTIMEDIA_EDIT -> {
1134→                if (resultCode != RESULT_CANCELED) {
1135→                    val col = getColUnsafe
1136→                    val extras = data!!.extras ?: return
1137→                    val index = extras.getInt(MultimediaEditFieldActivity.EXTRA_RESULT_FIELD_INDEX)
1138→                    val field = extras[MultimediaEditFieldActivity.EXTRA_RESULT_FIELD] as IField? ?: return
1139→                    if (field.type != EFieldType.TEXT && (field.imagePath == null && field.audioPath == null)) {
1140→                        Timber.i("field imagePath and audioPath are both null")
1141→                        return
1142→                    }
1143→                    val note = getCurrentMultimediaEditableNote(col)
1144→                    note.setField(index, field)
1145→                    val fieldEditText = mEditFields!![index]
1146→                    // Import field media
1147→                    // This goes before setting formattedValue to update
1148→                    // media paths with the checksum when they have the same name
1149→                    NoteService.importMediaToDirectory(col, field)
1150→                    // Completely replace text for text fields (because current text was passed in)
1151→                    val formattedValue = field.formattedValue
1152→                    if (field.type === EFieldType.TEXT) {
1153→                        fieldEditText!!.setText(formattedValue)
1154→                    } else if (fieldEditText!!.text != null) {
1155→                        insertStringInField(fieldEditText, formattedValue)
1156→                    }
1157→                    changed = true
1158→                }
1159→            }
1160→            REQUEST_TEMPLATE_EDIT -> {
1161→                // Model can change regardless of exit type - update ourselves and CardBrowser
1162→                mReloadRequired = true
1163→                mEditorNote!!.reloadModel()
1164→                if (mCurrentEditedCard == null || !mEditorNote!!.cids()
1165→                    .contains(mCurrentEditedCard!!.id)
1166→                ) {
1167→                    if (!addNote) {
1168→                        /* This can occur, for example, if the
1169→                             * card type was deleted or if the note
1170→                             * type was changed without moving this
1171→                             * card to another type. */
1172→                        Timber.d("onActivityResult() template edit return - current card is gone, close note editor")
1173→                        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.template_for_current_card_deleted))
1174→                        closeNoteEditor()
1175→                    } else {
1176→                        Timber.d("onActivityResult() template edit return, in add mode, just re-display")
1177→                    }
1178→                } else {
1179→                    Timber.d("onActivityResult() template edit return - current card exists")
1180→                    // reload current card - the template ordinals are possibly different post-edit
1181→                    mCurrentEditedCard = getColUnsafe.getCard(mCurrentEditedCard!!.id)
1182→                    updateCards(mEditorNote!!.model())
1183→                }
1184→            }
1185→        }
1186→    }

1188→    /** Appends a string at the selection point, or appends to the end if not in focus  */
1189→    @VisibleForTesting
1190→    fun insertStringInField(fieldEditText: EditText?, formattedValue: String?) {
1191→        if (fieldEditText!!.hasFocus()) {
1192→            // Crashes if start > end, although this is fine for a selection via keyboard.
1193→            val start = fieldEditText.selectionStart
1194→            val end = fieldEditText.selectionEnd
1195→            fieldEditText.text.replace(min(start, end), max(start, end), formattedValue)
1196→        } else {
1197→            fieldEditText.text.append(formattedValue)
1198→        }
1199→    }

1234→    private fun populateEditFields(type: FieldChangeType, editModelMode: Boolean) {
1235→        val editLines = mFieldState.loadFieldEditLines(type)
1236→        mFieldsLayoutContainer!!.removeAllViews()
1237→        mCustomViewIds.clear()
1238→        mEditFields = LinkedList()
1239→
1240→        // Use custom font if selected from preferences
1241→        var customTypeface: Typeface? = null
1242→        val preferences = baseContext.sharedPrefs()
1243→        val customFont = preferences.getString("browserEditorFont", "")
1244→        if ("" != customFont) {
1245→            customTypeface = AnkiFont.getTypeface(this, customFont!!)
1246→        }
1247→        var previous: FieldEditLine? = null
1248→        mCustomViewIds.ensureCapacity(editLines.size)
1249→        for (i in editLines.indices) {
1250→            val editLineView = editLines[i]
1251→            mCustomViewIds.add(editLineView.id)
1252→            val newEditText = editLineView.editText
1253→            newEditText.setImagePasteListener { editText: EditText?, uri: Uri? ->
1254→                onImagePaste(
1255→                    editText!!,
1256→                    uri!!
1257→                )
1258→            }
1259→            if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT < Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
1260→                if (i == 0) {
1261→                    findViewById<View>(R.id.note_deck_spinner).nextFocusForwardId = newEditText.id
1262→                }
1263→                if (previous != null) {
1264→                    previous.lastViewInTabOrder.nextFocusForwardId = newEditText.id
1265→                }
1266→            }
1267→            previous = editLineView
1268→            editLineView.setEnableAnimation(animationEnabled())
1269→
1270→            // Use custom implementation of ActionMode.Callback customize selection and insert menus
1271→            editLineView.setActionModeCallbacks(ActionModeCallback(newEditText))
1272→            editLineView.setTypeface(customTypeface)
1273→            editLineView.setHintLocale(getHintLocaleForField(editLineView.name))
1274→            initFieldEditText(newEditText, i, !editModelMode)
1275→            mEditFields!!.add(newEditText)
1276→            val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
1277→            if (prefs.getInt(PREF_NOTE_EDITOR_FONT_SIZE, -1) > 0) {
1278→                newEditText.textSize = prefs.getInt(PREF_NOTE_EDITOR_FONT_SIZE, -1).toFloat()
1279→            }
1280→            newEditText.setCapitalize(prefs.getBoolean(PREF_NOTE_EDITOR_CAPITALIZE, true))
1281→            val mediaButton = editLineView.mediaButton
1282→            val toggleStickyButton = editLineView.toggleSticky
1283→            // Load icons from attributes
1284→            val icons = Themes.getResFromAttr(
1285→                this,
1286→                intArrayOf(R.attr.attachFileImage, R.attr.upDownImage, R.attr.toggleStickyImage)
1287→            )
1288→            // Make the icon change between media icon and switch field icon depending on whether editing note type
1289→            if (editModelMode && allowFieldRemapping()) {
1290→                // Allow remapping if originally more than two fields
1291→                mediaButton.setBackgroundResource(icons[1])
1292→                setRemapButtonListener(mediaButton, i)
1293→                toggleStickyButton.setBackgroundResource(0)
1294→            } else if (editModelMode && !allowFieldRemapping()) {
1295→                mediaButton.setBackgroundResource(0)
1296→                toggleStickyButton.setBackgroundResource(0)
1297→            } else {
1298→                // Use media editor button if not changing note type
1299→                mediaButton.setBackgroundResource(icons[0])
1300→                setMMButtonListener(mediaButton, i)
1301→                if (addNote) {
1302→                    // toggle sticky button
1303→                    toggleStickyButton.setBackgroundResource(icons[2])
1304→                    setToggleStickyButtonListener(toggleStickyButton, i)
1305→                } else {
1306→                    toggleStickyButton.setBackgroundResource(0)
1307→                }
1308→            }
1309→            if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT < Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
1310→                previous.lastViewInTabOrder.nextFocusForwardId = R.id.CardEditorTagButton
1311→            }
1312→            mediaButton.contentDescription =
1313→                getString(R.string.multimedia_editor_attach_mm_content, editLineView.name)
1314→            toggleStickyButton.contentDescription =
1315→                getString(R.string.note_editor_toggle_sticky, editLineView.name)
1316→            mFieldsLayoutContainer!!.addView(editLineView)
1317→        }
1318→    }

1444→    private fun setRemapButtonListener(remapButton: ImageButton?, newFieldIndex: Int) {
1445→        remapButton!!.setOnClickListener { v: View? ->
1446→            Timber.i("NoteEditor:: Remap button pressed for new field %d", newFieldIndex)
1447→            // Show list of fields from the original note which we can map to
1448→            val popup = PopupMenu(this@NoteEditor, v!!)
1449→            val items = mEditorNote!!.items()
1450→            for (i in items.indices) {
1451→                popup.menu.add(Menu.NONE, i, Menu.NONE, items[i][0])
1452→            }
1453→            // Add "nothing" at the end of the list
1454→            popup.menu.add(Menu.NONE, items.size, Menu.NONE, R.string.nothing)
1455→            popup.setOnMenuItemClickListener { item: MenuItem ->
1456→                // Get menu item id
1457→                val idx = item.itemId
1458→                Timber.i("NoteEditor:: User chose to remap to old field %d", idx)
1459→                // Retrieve any existing mappings between newFieldIndex and idx
1460→                val previousMapping = MapUtil.getKeyByValue(mModelChangeFieldMap!!, newFieldIndex)
1461→                val mappingConflict = mModelChangeFieldMap!![idx]
1462→                // Update the mapping depending on any conflicts
1463→                if (idx == items.size && previousMapping != null) {
1464→                    // Remove the previous mapping if None selected
1465→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!!.remove(previousMapping)
1466→                } else if (idx < items.size && mappingConflict != null && previousMapping != null && newFieldIndex != mappingConflict) {
1467→                    // Swap the two mappings if there was a conflict and previous mapping
1468→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!![previousMapping] = mappingConflict
1469→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!![idx] = newFieldIndex
1470→                } else if (idx < items.size && mappingConflict != null) {
1471→                    // Set the conflicting field to None if no previous mapping to swap into it
1472→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!!.remove(previousMapping)
1473→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!![idx] = newFieldIndex
1474→                } else if (idx < items.size) {
1475→                    // Can simply set the new mapping if no conflicts
1476→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!![idx] = newFieldIndex
1477→                }
1478→                // Reload the fields
1479→                updateFieldsFromMap(currentlySelectedNotetype)
1480→                true
1481→            }
1482→            popup.show()
1483→        }
1484→    }

1493→    private fun initFieldEditText(editText: FieldEditText?, index: Int, enabled: Boolean) {
1494→        // Listen for changes in the first field so we can re-check duplicate status.
1495→        editText!!.addTextChangedListener(EditFieldTextWatcher(index))
1496→        if (index == 0) {
1497→            editText.onFocusChangeListener = OnFocusChangeListener { _: View?, hasFocus: Boolean ->
1498→                try {
1499→                    if (hasFocus) {
1500→                        // we only want to decorate when we lose focus
1501→                        return@OnFocusChangeListener
1502→                    }
1503→                    val currentFieldStrings = currentFieldStrings
1504→                    if (currentFieldStrings.size != 2 || currentFieldStrings[1]!!.isNotEmpty()) {
1505→                        // we only decorate on 2-field cards while second field is still empty
1506→                        return@OnFocusChangeListener
1507→                    }
1508→                    val firstField = currentFieldStrings[0]
1509→                    val decoratedText = NoteFieldDecorator.aplicaHuevo(firstField)
1510→                    if (decoratedText != firstField) {
1511→                        // we only apply the decoration if it is actually different from the first field
1512→                        setFieldValueFromUi(1, decoratedText)
1513→                    }
1514→                } catch (e: Exception) {
1515→                    Timber.w(e, "Unable to decorate text field")
1516→                }
1517→            }
1518→        }
1519→
1520→        // Sets the background color of disabled EditText.
1521→        if (!enabled) {
1522→            editText.setBackgroundColor(
1523→                Themes.getColorFromAttr(
1524→                    this@NoteEditor,
1525→                    R.attr.editTextBackgroundColor
1526→                )
1527→            )
1528→        }
1529→        editText.isEnabled = enabled
1530→    }

1548→    private fun setEditFieldTexts(contents: String?) {
1549→        var fields: Array<String>? = null
1550→        val len: Int
1551→        if (contents == null) {
1552→            len = 0
1553→        } else {
1554→            fields = Utils.splitFields(contents)
1555→            len = fields.size
1556→        }
1557→        for (i in mEditFields!!.indices) {
1558→            if (i < len) {
1559→                mEditFields!![i]!!.setText(fields!![i])

1566→    private fun setDuplicateFieldStyles() {
1567→        val field = mEditFields!![0]
1568→        // Keep copy of current internal value for this field.
1569→        val oldValue = mEditorNote!!.fields[0]
1570→        // Update the field in the Note so we can run a dupe check on it.
1571→        updateField(field)
1572→        // 1 is empty, 2 is dupe, null is neither.
1573→        val dupeCode = mEditorNote!!.dupeOrEmpty()
1574→        // Change bottom line color of text field
1575→        if (dupeCode == DupeOrEmpty.DUPE) {
1576→            field!!.setDupeStyle()
1577→        } else {
1578→            field!!.setDefaultStyle()
1579→        }
1580→        // Put back the old value so we don't interfere with modification detection
1581→        mEditorNote!!.values()[0] = oldValue
1582→    }

1600→    private fun setDid(note: Note?) {
1601→        // If the target deck ID has already been set, we use that value and avoid trying to
1602→        // determine what it should be again. An existing value means we are resuming the activity
1603→        // where the target deck was already decided by the user.
1604→        if (deckId != 0L) {
1605→            mDeckSpinnerSelection!!.selectDeckById(deckId, false)
1606→            return
1607→        }
1608→        if (note == null || addNote || mCurrentEditedCard == null) {
1609→            val model = getColUnsafe.notetypes.current()
1610→            if (getColUnsafe.config.getBool(ConfigKey.Bool.ADDING_DEFAULTS_TO_CURRENT_DECK)) {
1611→                deckId = getColUnsafe.config.get(CURRENT_DECK) ?: 1
1612→                if (getColUnsafe.decks.isDyn(deckId)) {
1613→                    /*
1614→                     * If the deck in mCurrentDid is a filtered (dynamic) deck, then we can't create cards in it,
1615→                     * and we set mCurrentDid to the Default deck. Otherwise, we keep the number that had been
1616→                     * selected previously in the activity.
1617→                     */
1618→                    deckId = 1
1619→                }
1620→            } else {
1621→                deckId = model.did
1622→            }
1623→        } else {
1624→            deckId = mCurrentEditedCard!!.did
1625→        }
1626→        mDeckSpinnerSelection!!.selectDeckById(deckId, false)
1627→    }

1655→    private fun updateToolbar() {
1656→        val editorLayout = findViewById<View>(R.id.note_editor_layout)
1657→        val bottomMargin =
1658→            if (shouldHideToolbar()) {
1659→                0
1660→            } else {
1661→                resources.getDimension(R.dimen.note_editor_toolbar_height)
1662→                    .toInt()
1663→            }
1664→        val params = editorLayout.layoutParams as MarginLayoutParams
1665→        params.bottomMargin = bottomMargin
1666→        editorLayout.layoutParams = params
1667→        if (shouldHideToolbar()) {
1668→            toolbar.visibility = View.GONE
1669→            return
1670→        } else {
1671→            toolbar.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1672→        }
1673→        toolbar.clearCustomItems()
1674→        if (mEditorNote!!.model().isCloze) {
1675→            val clozeFormatter = Toolbar.TextFormatter { s: String ->
1676→                val stringFormat = Toolbar.StringFormat()
1677→                val prefix = "{{c$nextClozeIndex::"
1678→                stringFormat.result = "$prefix$s}}"
1679→                if (s.isEmpty()) {
1680→                    stringFormat.selectionStart = prefix.length
1681→                    stringFormat.selectionEnd = prefix.length
1682→                } else {
1683→                    stringFormat.selectionStart = 0
1684→                    stringFormat.selectionEnd = stringFormat.result.length
1685→                }
1686→                stringFormat
1687→            }
1688→
1689→            val clozeDrawable = ResourcesCompat.getDrawable(resources, R.drawable.ic_cloze_black_24dp, null)
1690→            clozeDrawable!!.setTint(Themes.getColorFromAttr(this@NoteEditor, R.attr.toolbarIconColor))
1691→            val clozeButton = toolbar.insertItem(0, clozeDrawable, Runnable { toolbar.onFormat(clozeFormatter) })
1692→            clozeButton.contentDescription = resources.getString(R.string.insert_cloze)
1693→            TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(clozeButton, resources.getString(R.string.insert_cloze))
1694→        }
1695→        val buttons = toolbarButtons
1696→        for (b in buttons) {
1697→            // 0th button shows as '1' and is Ctrl + 1
1698→            val visualIndex = b.index + 1
1699→            var text = visualIndex.toString()
1700→            if (b.buttonText.isNotEmpty()) {
1701→                text = b.buttonText
1702→            }
1703→            val bmp = toolbar.createDrawableForString(text)
1704→            val v = toolbar.insertItem(0, bmp, b.toFormatter())
1705→            v.contentDescription = text
1706→
1707→            // Allow Ctrl + 1...Ctrl + 0 for item 10.
1708→            v.tag = (visualIndex % 10).toString()
1709→            v.setOnLongClickListener {
1710→                displayEditToolbarDialog(b)
1711→                true
1712→            }
1713→        }
1714→
1715→        // Let the user add more buttons (always at the end).
1716→        // Sets the add custom tag icon color.
1717→        val drawable = ResourcesCompat.getDrawable(resources, R.drawable.ic_add_toolbar_icon, null)
1718→        drawable!!.setTint(Themes.getColorFromAttr(this@NoteEditor, R.attr.toolbarIconColor))
1719→        val addButton = toolbar.insertItem(0, drawable, Runnable { displayAddToolbarDialog() })
1720→        addButton.contentDescription = resources.getString(R.string.add_toolbar_item)
1721→        TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(addButton, resources.getString(R.string.add_toolbar_item))
1722→    }

1853→    /** Update the list of card templates for current note type  */
1854→    private fun updateCards(model: JSONObject?) {
1855→        Timber.d("updateCards()")
1856→        val tmpls = model!!.getJSONArray("tmpls")
1857→        var cardsList = StringBuilder()
1858→        // Build comma separated list of card names
1859→        Timber.d("updateCards() template count is %s", tmpls.length())
1860→        for (i in 0 until tmpls.length()) {
1861→            var name = tmpls.getJSONObject(i).optString("name")
1862→            // If more than one card, and we have an existing card, underline existing card
1863→            if (!addNote && tmpls.length() > 1 && model === mEditorNote!!.model() && mCurrentEditedCard != null && mCurrentEditedCard!!.template()
1864→                .optString("name") == name
1865→            ) {
1866→                name = "<u>$name</u>"
1867→            }
1868→            cardsList.append(name)
1869→            if (i < tmpls.length() - 1) {
1870→                cardsList.append(", ")
1871→            }
1872→        }
1873→        // Make cards list red if the number of cards is being reduced
1874→        if (!addNote && tmpls.length() < mEditorNote!!.model().getJSONArray("tmpls").length()) {
1875→            cardsList = StringBuilder("<font color='red'>$cardsList</font>")
1876→        }
1877→        mCardsButton!!.text = HtmlCompat.fromHtml(
1878→            resources.getString(R.string.CardEditorCards, cardsList.toString()),
1879→            HtmlCompat.FROM_HTML_MODE_LEGACY
1880→        )
1881→    }

1883→    private fun updateField(field: FieldEditText?): Boolean {
1884→        val fieldContent = field!!.text?.toString() ?: ""
1885→        val correctedFieldContent = NoteService.convertToHtmlNewline(fieldContent, shouldReplaceNewlines())
1886→        if (mEditorNote!!.values()[field.ord] != correctedFieldContent) {
1887→            mEditorNote!!.values()[field.ord] = correctedFieldContent
1888→            return true
1889→        }
1890→        return false
1891→    }

1925→    private inner class SetNoteTypeListener : OnItemSelectedListener {
1926→        override fun onItemSelected(parent: AdapterView<*>?, view: View?, pos: Int, id: Long) {
1927→            // If a new column was selected then change the key used to map from mCards to the column TextView
1928→            // Timber.i("NoteEditor:: onItemSelected() fired on mNoteTypeSpinner");
1929→            val oldModelId = getColUnsafe.notetypes.current().getLong("id")
1930→            val newId = mAllModelIds!![pos]
1931→            Timber.i("Changing note type to '%d", newId)
1932→            if (oldModelId != newId) {
1933→                val model = getColUnsafe.notetypes.get(newId)
1934→                if (model == null) {
1935→                    Timber.w("New model %s not found, not changing note type", newId)
1936→                    return
1937→                }
1938→                getColUnsafe.notetypes.setCurrent(model)
1939→                val currentDeck = getColUnsafe.decks.current()
1940→                currentDeck.put("mid", newId)
1941→                getColUnsafe.decks.save(currentDeck)
1942→                // Update deck
1943→                if (!getColUnsafe.config.getBool(ConfigKey.Bool.ADDING_DEFAULTS_TO_CURRENT_DECK)) {
1944→                    deckId = model.optLong("did", Consts.DEFAULT_DECK_ID)
1945→                }
1946→                refreshNoteData(FieldChangeType.changeFieldCount(shouldReplaceNewlines()))
1947→                setDuplicateFieldStyles()
1948→                mDeckSpinnerSelection!!.updateDeckPosition(deckId)
1949→            }
1950→        }
1951→
1952→        override fun onNothingSelected(parent: AdapterView<*>?) {
1953→            // Do Nothing
1954→        }
1955→    }
1956→
1957→    /* Uses only if mCurrentEditedCard is set, so from reviewer or card browser.*/
1958→    private inner class EditNoteTypeListener : OnItemSelectedListener {
1959→        override fun onItemSelected(parent: AdapterView<*>?, view: View?, pos: Int, id: Long) {
1960→            // Get the current model
1961→            val noteModelId = mCurrentEditedCard!!.model().getLong("id")
1962→            // Get new model
1963→            val newModel = getColUnsafe.notetypes.get(mAllModelIds!![pos])
1964→            if (newModel == null) {
1965→                Timber.w("newModel %s not found", mAllModelIds!![pos])
1966→                return
1967→            }
1968→            // Configure the interface according to whether note type is getting changed or not
1969→            if (mAllModelIds!![pos] != noteModelId) {
1970→                @KotlinCleanup("Check if this ever happens")
1971→                val tmpls = try {
1972→                    newModel.getJSONArray("tmpls")
1973→                } catch (e: Exception) {
1974→                    Timber.w("error in obtaining templates from model %s", mAllModelIds!![pos])
1975→                    return
1976→                }
1977→                // Initialize mapping between fields of old model -> new model
1978→                val itemsLength = mEditorNote!!.items().size
1979→                mModelChangeFieldMap = HashUtil.HashMapInit(itemsLength)
1980→                for (i in 0 until itemsLength) {
1981→                    mModelChangeFieldMap!![i] = i
1982→                }
1983→                // Initialize mapping between cards new model -> old model
1984→                val templatesLength = tmpls.length()
1985→                mModelChangeCardMap = HashUtil.HashMapInit(templatesLength)
1986→                for (i in 0 until templatesLength) {
1987→                    if (i < mEditorNote!!.numberOfCards()) {
1988→                        mModelChangeCardMap!![i] = i
1989→                    } else {
1990→                        mModelChangeCardMap!![i] = null
1991→                    }
1992→                }
1993→                // Update the field text edits based on the default mapping just assigned
1994→                updateFieldsFromMap(newModel)
1995→                // Don't let the user change any other values at the same time as changing note type
1996→                mSelectedTags = mEditorNote!!.tags
1997→                updateTags()
1998→                findViewById<View>(R.id.CardEditorTagButton).isEnabled = false
1999→                // ((LinearLayout) findViewById(R.id.CardEditorCardsButton)).setEnabled(false);
2000→                mDeckSpinnerSelection!!.setEnabledActionBarSpinner(false)

 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.view.MotionEvent
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import android.widget.ArrayAdapter
 22→import android.widget.Spinner
 23→import android.widget.TextView
 24→import androidx.appcompat.app.ActionBar
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck.Companion.fromCollection
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.widgets.DeckDropDownAdapter
 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.*
 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.FragmentManagerSupplier
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.asFragmentManagerSupplier
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}
220→

 70→open class RobolectricTest : AndroidTest {
 71→
 72→    @Suppress("PLATFORM_CLASS_MAPPED_TO_KOTLIN")
 73→    private fun Any.wait(timeMs: Long) = (this as Object).wait(timeMs)
 74→
 75→    private val mControllersForCleanup = ArrayList<ActivityController<*>>()
 76→
 77→    protected fun saveControllerForCleanup(controller: ActivityController<*>) {
 78→        mControllersForCleanup.add(controller)
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    protected open fun useInMemoryDatabase(): Boolean {
 82→        return true
 83→    }
 84→
 85→    @get:Rule
 86→    val mTaskScheduler = TaskSchedulerRule()
 87→
 88→    /** Allows [com.ichi2.testutils.Flaky] to annotate tests in subclasses */
 89→    @get:Rule
 90→    val ignoreFlakyTests = IgnoreFlakyTestsInCIRule()
 91→
 92→    @Before
 93→    @CallSuper
 94→    open fun setUp() {
 95→        TimeManager.resetWith(MockTime(2020, 7, 7, 7, 0, 0, 0, 10))
 96→
 97→        ChangeManager.clearSubscribers()
 98→
 99→        // resolved issues with the collection being reused if useInMemoryDatabase is false
100→        CollectionHelper.instance.setColForTests(null)
101→
102→        maybeSetupBackend()
103→
104→        // If you want to see the Android logging (from Timber), you need to set it up here
105→        ShadowLog.stream = System.out
106→
107→        Storage.setUseInMemory(useInMemoryDatabase())
108→
109→        // Reset static variable for custom tabs failure.
110→        CustomTabActivityHelper.resetFailed()
111→
112→        // See: #6140 - This global ideally shouldn't exist, but it will cause crashes if set.
113→        DialogHandler.discardMessage()
114→
115→        // BUG: We do not reset the MetaDB
116→        MetaDB.closeDB()
117→    }
118→
119→    protected open fun useLegacyHelper(): Boolean {
120→        return false
121→    }
122→
123→    protected fun getHelperFactory(): SupportSQLiteOpenHelper.Factory =
124→        if (useInMemoryDatabase()) {
125→            Timber.w("Using in-memory database for test. Collection should not be re-opened")
126→            InMemorySQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
127→        } else {
128→            FrameworkSQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
129→        }
130→
131→    @After
132→    @CallSuper
133→    open fun tearDown() {
134→        // If you don't clean up your ActivityControllers you will get OOM errors
135→        for (controller in mControllersForCleanup) {
136→            Timber.d("Calling destroy on controller %s", controller.get().toString())
137→            try {
138→                controller.destroy()
139→            } catch (e: Exception) {
140→                // Any exception here is likely because the test code already destroyed it, which is fine
141→                // No exception here should halt test execution since tests are over anyway.
142→            }
143→        }
144→        mControllersForCleanup.clear()
145→
146→        try {
147→            if (CollectionHelper.instance.colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
148→                CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!.debugEnsureNoOpenPointers()
149→            }
150→            // If you don't tear down the database you'll get unexpected IllegalStateExceptions related to connections
151→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("RobolectricTest: End")
152→        } catch (ex: BackendException) {
153→            if ("CollectionNotOpen" == ex.message) {
154→                Timber.w(ex, "Collection was already disposed - may have been a problem")
155→            } else {
156→                throw ex
157→            }
158→        } finally {
159→            // After every test make sure the CollectionHelper is no longer overridden (done for null testing)
160→            disableNullCollection()
161→
162→            // called on each AnkiDroidApp.onCreate(), and spams the build
163→            // there is no onDestroy(), so call it here.
164→            Timber.uprootAll()
165→
166→            TimeManager.reset()
167→        }
168→        Dispatchers.resetMain()
169→        runBlocking { CollectionManager.discardBackend() }
170→    }

199→     */
200→    protected fun getMaterialDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
201→        val dialog: MaterialDialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
202→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {

216→    protected fun getAlertDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {

228→    // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
229→    companion object {
230→        private var mBackground = true
231→
232→        // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
233→        fun advanceRobolectricLooper() {
234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return
236→            }
237→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()
239→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
240→        }
241→
242→        /**
243→         * * Causes all of the [Runnable]s that have been scheduled to run while advancing the clock to the start time of the last scheduled Runnable.
244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI
253→                // This line was added and may or may not make a difference
254→                runToEndOfTasks()
255→            }
256→        }
257→
258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {
265→                Thread.sleep(500)
266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }
269→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
270→        }
271→
272→        /** This can probably be implemented in a better manner  */
273→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
274→        protected fun waitForAsyncTasksToComplete() {
275→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
276→        }
277→
278→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
279→        protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(testClass: RobolectricTest, clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
280→            if (AbstractFlashcardViewer::class.java.isAssignableFrom(clazz!!)) {
281→                // fixes 'Don't know what to do with dataSource...' inside Sounds.kt
282→                // solution from https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/issues/4673
283→                ShadowMediaPlayer.setMediaInfoProvider {
284→                    ShadowMediaPlayer.MediaInfo(1, 0)
285→                }
286→            }
287→            val controller = Robolectric.buildActivity(clazz, i)
288→                .create().start().resume().visible()
289→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
290→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
291→            testClass.saveControllerForCleanup(controller)
292→            return controller.get()
293→        }
294→    }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {
394→        val model = col.notetypes.byName((name)!!)
395→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Could not find model '$name'")
396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.
397→        // Saves a database trip afterwards.
398→        val n = col.newNote(model)
399→        for ((i, field) in fields.withIndex()) {
400→            n.setField(i, field)
401→        }
402→        check(col.addNote(n) != 0) { "Could not add note: {${fields.joinToString(separator = ", ")}}" }
403→        return n
404→    }
405→
406→    protected fun addNonClozeModel(name: String, fields: Array<String>, qfmt: String?, afmt: String?): String {
407→        val model = col.notetypes.newModel(name)
408→        for (field in fields) {
409→            col.notetypes.addFieldInNewModel(model, col.notetypes.newField(field))
410→        }
411→        val t = Notetypes.newTemplate("Card 1")
412→        t.put("qfmt", qfmt)
413→        t.put("afmt", afmt)
414→        col.notetypes.addTemplateInNewModel(model, t)
415→        col.notetypes.add(model)
416→        return name
417→    }

450→    /**
451→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
452→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
453→     * Example:
454→     * <pre>:
455→     *   assumeThat(1, is(1)); // passes
456→     *   foo(); // will execute
457→     *   assumeThat(0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
458→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
459→     * </pre>
460→     *
461→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
462→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
463→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
464→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
465→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
466→     */
467→    fun <T> assumeThat(actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
468→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
469→        Assume.assumeThat(actual, matcher)
470→    }

472→    /**
473→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
474→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
475→     * Example:
476→     * <pre>:
477→     *   assumeThat("alwaysPasses", 1, is(1)); // passes
478→     *   foo(); // will execute
479→     *   assumeThat("alwaysFails", 0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
480→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
481→     * </pre>
482→     *
483→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
484→     * @param actual the computed value being compared

486→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
487→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
488→     */
489→    fun <T> assumeThat(message: String?, actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
490→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
491→        Assume.assumeThat(message, actual, matcher)
492→    }

529→    private fun maybeSetupBackend() {
530→        try {
531→            targetContext
532→        } catch (exc: IllegalStateException) {
533→            // We must make sure not to load the backend library into a test running outside
534→            // the Robolectric classloader, or subsequent Robolectric tests that run in this
535→            // process will be unable to make calls into the backend.
536→            println("not annotated with junit, not setting up backend")
537→            return
538→        }
539→        try {
540→            RustBackendLoader.ensureSetup()
541→        } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
542→            if (e.message.toString().contains("library load disallowed by system policy")) {
543→                throw IllegalStateException(
544→                    """library load disallowed by system policy.
545→"To fix:
546→* Run the test such that the "developer cannot be verified" message appears
547→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
548→* Run the Test Again
549→* Apple Menu - System Preferences - Security & Privacy - General (tab) - Unlock Settings - Select Allow Anyway". 
550→    Button is underneath the text: "librsdroid.dylib was blocked from use because it is not from an identified developer"
551→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
552→* Test should execute correctly"""
553→                )
554→            }
555→            throw e
556→        }
557→    }

559→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
560→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
561→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
562→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
563→     * issues with using it at the moment:
564→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
565→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
566→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
567→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
568→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
569→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
570→     *
571→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
572→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
573→     * on separate threads/processes.
574→     * */
575→    fun runTest(
576→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
577→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
578→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
579→    ) {
580→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
581→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
582→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
583→            testBody()
584→        }
585→    }
586→}
